tweets.text:
Rene Mauricio Flores-Lue was convicted in Rock County, Minn., of fourth-degree criminal sexual conduct, after a plea deal. The prosecutor originally charged him with child rape.

His sentence? A mere two years and $285 in fees and fines.

With a final removal order on file, ICE special agents arrested him during a targeted operation in Luverne, Minn., April 14.

By the time we arrested him, he had already racked up an extensive criminal history, including multiple traffic offenses and a DUI conviction.
tweets.text_resolved:
Rene Mauricio Flores-Lue was convicted in Rock County, Minn., of fourth-degree criminal sexual conduct, after a plea deal. The prosecutor originally charged him with child rape.

His sentence? A mere two years and $285 in fees and fines.

With a final removal order on file, ICE special agents arrested him during a targeted operation in Luverne, Minn., April 14.

tweets.text:
The NYC district attorney called the deadly arson attack “an act of mass murder” after the suspect watched people jump from their burning apartments while sipping a beer.

Despite this horrific incident, NYC remains adamant it will not honor the ICE detainers that keep murderers and other criminals from returning to the city’s streets.

This goes beyond politics. It’s time to protect Americans.

tweets.text:
ICE ARRESTS FOREIGN FUGITIVE WANTED FOR HOMICIDE IN BRAZIL

ICE arrested Magno Jose Dos Santos, a criminal illegal alien and foreign fugitive wanted for homicide in Brazil, during a targeted operation in Worcester, MA, March 22.

He’s faced charges for violent crimes on multiple occasions in Massachusetts, but each was dismissed. 

Plus, in total, this ILLEGAL ALIEN has 12 motor vehicle citations in Massachusetts and Connecticut. Over a three-month span in 2024, police stopped him for motor vehicle violations nine times, but each time, they let him off with a warning.

tweets.text:
Police arrested Bryan Rafael Gomez, a criminal illegal alien from the Dominican Republic, April 4 in Worcester, Mass., for domestic abuse. ICE arrested him upon his release from jail in Worcester that same day.

A background check revealed Dominican Republic authorities issued a criminal arrest warrant against him for homicide in 2023.

He’ll stay in ICE custody pending removal proceedings.

tweets.text:
Rene Rosario-Miranda was convicted of statutory rape last September after being charged with child molestation. He was sentenced to 207 days in jail. That wasn’t his first run in with the law either — he also served a 364-day sentence stemming from a 2024 DUI conviction.

ICE special agents arrested him as he was leaving the Prairie Law Enforcement Center in Worthington, Minn., April 14.

tweets.text:
ICE Fugitive Operations arrested Altieris Chaves Paiva during a targeted operation April 5 near Falmouth, Mass., thanks to a tip.

Chaves Paiva, a criminal illegal alien from Brazil, is wanted in Brazil for HOMICIDE, but he has a criminal record in Massachusetts, too.

Police in Oak Bluffs arrested him in 2024 for drunk driving, negligent driving and another traffic offense, but he pleaded guilty to only negligent driving. He was given probation and continued to live freely in the community.

This fugitive will remain in ICE custody pending removal proceedings.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
When local authorities honor detainers, aliens can’t return to the community to reoffend and fewer ICE officers are needed.

Take Daniel Jesus Piedra-Fernandez, of Colombia, as an example:

👉He’s a violent aggravated felon with convictions for robbery, battery, burglary, property damage and domestic violence. 
👉He has a final order of removal and a recent arrest. 
👉ICE Salt Lake City took custody of him within the security of a local jail because officials there honored our detainer.

In a sanctuary jurisdiction, Piedra-Fernandez would have been released back into the community, and ICE would have to assemble a large team to apprehend him at-large.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Do you know that ICE’s VOICE office supports victims and families impacted by immigration-related crimes by:

• Helping victims follow and understand the immigration enforcement and removal process.
• Signing up victims to receive automated custody status information on criminal aliens in ICE custody.
• Opportunities to ensure victims can provide victim impact statements.
• Giving access to social service professionals and local resources.

➡️To learn more, visit our web page.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
QUEENS, N.Y. — District Attorney Melinda Katz announced April 9 that Roman Ceron Amatitla, a criminal illegal alien from Mexico, was charged with EIGHT COUNTS OF MURDER and ARSON after he allegedly deliberetely set a building on fire and watched it burn — killing four people, including a toddler, and injuring several  more.

ICE issued a detainer with the New York City Department of Corrections asking them to notify ICE if he is released and hold him until our officers can arrive.

Because the City of New York is a sanctuary jurisdiction, NYCDOC officials told our agency that they will not honor the detainer.
tweets.text_resolved:
QUEENS, N.Y. — District Attorney Melinda Katz announced April 9 that Roman Ceron Amatitla, a criminal illegal alien from Mexico, was charged with EIGHT COUNTS OF MURDER and ARSON after he allegedly deliberetely set a building on fire and watched it burn — killing four people, including a toddler, and injuring several  more.

ICE issued a detainer with the New York City Department of Corrections asking them to notify ICE if he is released and hold him until our officers can arrive.

Because the City of New York is a sanctuary jurisdiction, NYCDOC officials told our agency that they will not honor the detainer.
